---
status: draft
shortDescription: "Smaller types are never automatically converted into bigger ones without an explicit cast"
shortCorrection: "Smaller types can be automatically converted into bigger ones even without an explicit cast"
concepts:
  - Type
  # - Cast ?
related:
  # - id: LiteralNoExpression
  #   relation: Special case
inOtherPl:
  # - pl: Java
  #   id: NoAtomicExpression
  # - pl: Python
  #   id: NoAtomicExpression
spec:
  # - id: ecma11
  #   sections: ["11.8"]

  # from: ecma6

- id: ExpressionAsTree
  how: expression involving assignment of value of smaller type to variable of bigger type
references:
---

An explicit cast is required to store a value of a smaller type such as `short, byte` into a variable of a bigger type such as `int`.

frontmatter.shortDescription: Smaller types are never automatically converted into bigger ones without an explicit cast
frontmatter.shortCorrection: Smaller types can be automatically converted into bigger ones even without an explicit cast
